Hey everyone, this is Russell Brunson and I want to welcome you guys to episode number two of Funnel Hacker TV. Now this episode I am really, really excited for because I'm going to show you guys one of our Dotcom Secrets Inner Circle members who's also one of our Click Funnels All Stars who is going to be showing you guys behind the scenes of one of his sales funnels. And it's one that I've been watching ever since day one when he first started crafting and putting it together. And I've watched as he's gone from this little tiny company to this big, huge company where he's literally shipping out thousands and thousands of products every single day. In fact, on the outside you may think that he's selling physical products, but if you look at what he explains during this interview, you're going to see that this is actually the blueprint that he's created to get to $400,000 per month in digital continuity programs. So I'm excited to show you guys that here during episode number two. We are going to be talking with Trey Lou Ellen who is someone that I've just become a huge fan and a friend of over the last year or so. And I had a chance to watch him use Click Funnels to take this company. It was just starting and blow it up massively. It's been so funny. He always tags me on pictures on Facebook where he's shipping out just like trucks full of all these crazy items. And then we're going to bring Trey on the line and have him walk you through all the pages and how it all works. So it's going to be awesome. All right. So what I want to do is I want to kind of show you the process. Now with Trey, what he's doing is he's selling a bunch of low ticket free plus shipping products to get people to become customers. And so he's in the gun market. So he sells free gun targets. He sells a wolf cleaning oil, which is the one we're going to kind of show you guys here during this episode. He has all sorts of other little gadgets. He's always coming with new front end products to give away for free plus shipping to get people into his funnel. So join his continuity program. And so he's going to show you behind the scenes really in depth one of those funnels, but then you'll see kind of how it works over and over and over again. So this is kind of how the funnel, how this funnel he's going to show you works. So the first thing that he's got here is you'll see on his landing page is there's going to be an offer. And the offer he's going to show you is for, if I can draw this well, there's wolf cleaning oil and a wolf oil for your guns. He's got a big old button here and there's some copy down here below. And when they click on this button to get their free bottle, there's the pop up that comes up. Then ask for their name and their email after they put that in, then take some over here to the order form. Now one really cool thing he did the order form, I'd never seen something to before and never even crossed my mind. If you watch episode number one, I talked about the order form bumps, what we're doing on our order forms, but this was really kind of brilliant. What he found is that people who are buying gun targets or they're buying wolf oil or they're buying any of his free plus shipping front end offers, a lot of times they want more than one. And so we took this order form, just kind of two separate here. They put their credit card and he added in some radio buttons. The first one was like, hey, if you want the wolf bottle, this is the one bottle here. Or if you want, you can choose two bottles. Or you can choose three or you can choose that four or he's got six. So people can come and they can choose which option they want. They say, hey, I want four bottles as long as I'm here. And from that, he's able to get way more front end revenue before the card even happens. And so he'll kind of walk you through that process, but it's brilliant, especially if you're selling something that people are going to want more of. You can just on the order form, give them the option and the ability to order more right there, which is kind of cool. So that's kind of the first step here in the process. Now this, you guys, where the magic happens, I'm going to show it to you. And first I'm guessing some of you guys are going to miss it. Changes an upsell that complements the first front end thing and gets them close to break even and boom, more people keep falling through here. Okay? That's the power and what he's doing, you guys, is he can take the same model and replicate it over and over and over again, all with one major goal, which is to feed people into the exact same continuity program. This is the goal and the focus of his company, of this funnel, every funnel he's creating. Now in the interview, Trey talks about how his company, they've got a goal of launch four sales funnels every single month. So every single week they're looking at this and launching a new one. Okay? And they're like, why are they doing that? Why don't they choose one funnel and just focus on it? The reality is, and you'll notice this when you start getting more mass marketing, you start growing, is that one funnel, people, doesn't matter how good your offer is, eventually it'll get what we call offer fatigue. Meaning everybody on Facebook who's a gun addict has a senior free target offer, right? Everyone who's into internet marketing has seen my free book offer. So we've got to rotate offers. We've got to have new things coming out that are exciting so that you can get new people to raise their hand or people who may have seen the past to now raise their hand or people who bought this whole thing. But for some reason, they didn't take this upsell. Okay? They said, no, I'm not going to take that upsell. And they see it the second time and the third time. And eventually one of these times they're going to say, you know what? I keep seeing this thing. It looks awesome. Now I'm going to be in. Okay? And so that's really the strategy behind how this funnel works. Now, I want you guys to think about this because after he kind of walked me through here, I really start focusing on my continuity program. Like I've got a really good continuity program. Some of you guys may be members of Dotcom Secrets Labs monthly. We've got an amazing offer, but we've been focusing on one funnel, one offer to get people in, right? And then I started thinking, man, what else could I do? What pieces of my continuity program can I pull out, create a new fronted offer that comes out here at the very beginning and the upsell now takes people into my core continuity. Okay? That's been kind of a big factor next in your shipping details. So that's a, that's the double opt-in. That's actually worked pretty well for us versus the, just like, you know, having the opt-in below the button. So they type in their name and email and then they make it to this beautiful order page. And we've worked tremendously hard on just like ramping and changing up the order form to get more and more conversions. Like we'll do like many split tests. Like I remember one split test was just making this the word free bold and it got like a percentage increase, a percent order bump on our order page. Also giving more value. Okay. Very well. So we wanted to also write a headline here. So it's easy to read on mobile, but if they are using desktop, the banner sells it as well. So there's a reason that we do both there. I mean, I can get into some of the things that we changed here, but for the most part, it's just free oil. If they want two ounces, four ounces or six ounces, we're just upping the price. So once 5.95, they want double the price. If they want four, it's quadruple the price and so on and so forth with the prices. What percentage takes that? This is unique. I've never seen anyone besides you do inside of ClickFunnels, which is really cool. So talk about what percentage takes what or how, something cool about that. Yep. So 50% take the first one, 30% will take the second, about 12 will take the rest. So it doesn't matter if we have, we've been testing 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. We've been testing 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 10. So it's always 50% are taken the first, 30% are always taken the second, and then the rest of the percentages are usually scaled out like a ladder. So it's just, that's where it is. So what's interesting though, Russell is like when I see someone take the six ounce or if they'll take a 10 ounce, I know that's telling me that there's people out there that want more. So I'll go back to the order form and I'll create, okay, well there's people taking the 10 ounce. So is there people that want 20 ounces? Yes, there is. Okay. Well, what about 30 ounces, right? Like I keep upping it to see all that stuff. So it's really interesting. So they're just basically paying for shipping. I believe the oil in our case, like some number wise, I think the oil costs us like two bucks and then it costs us three something to ship it. So we're right break even at the product, but obviously the cost of getting a customer is usually around 15 to 20 bucks. So that's where we're at within the funnel. That's cool. Didn't you do some tests on your button there too? Yes. We are always, so I learned this from Sean Lynam. So we always are again telling them what they're ordering in the button. You know, the psychology that like I've seen is no one likes to pay for anything. They like to receive stuff. So we've really changed up the order form in that way in that regard. So like shipping address, used to say billing address. So billing refers to you're going to charge me. Step three used to say checkout. We changed that to say product selection. So anything that refers to or the button that says buy now, anything that refers to buying, we changed. And now it's just we're just letting them say, oh, I'm receiving this, like we're giving this, this is something you're receiving. So once we started changing that around, like our order form went from around four or five percent and to now it's about 12 to 13 percent on a conversion just because of that feeling. It's really cool. It's like such a subtle thing. So we're just confirming them on the button. You do. It's so different. I mean, take a look at, you know, when you're shopping at a mall, you're going and grabbing stuff like right? It's already in your possession. Like, oh, I get to have this. Yeah. So we're talking about your chat box too. I know we talked about some of the cool stuff that that does as well. Yeah. I mean, I can go in as depth as you want. So the chat box is super important. There's three things on here. One you can't see. First is the chat box. If you don't have a chat box, you're crazy. This thing costs us $5 a month. Guys, secondly is our phone number. I had, I had somebody call in. So here's why the phone number is so important. This timer down here, we had it set to hit zero and then it would stay at zero for two days. We had a guy call and he's like, Hey, can I still get the wolf oil? I was like, Yeah, why would you think you couldn't get the wolf oil? He's like, Well, the timer says zero. So I thought it was thought I couldn't order anymore. I was like, Oh my gosh, are you freaking serious? Like how many other people thought that with landing on the page, like coming back from an email follow up or something and then timer says zero and didn't order because they thought, Oh, the time ran out. I guess I can't order anymore. It's like, Wow. So it's like, we're learning so many things just by the phone, by people calling in and a great example, a client of mine, he had a lady call in and she's like, Hey, I see I can only order four, four items here. He's like, Yeah, she's like, Well, actually, I want 10. He's like, Oh, geez. So if he didn't have that phone number, she would only ordered four, not the 10. So he made, he made more money because of the phone number. The other thing that we use that's not shown here is mouse flow. Mouse flow is 20 bucks a month. It's really great for when you're starting out on click funnels because it's 20 bucks a month. It does a thousand recordings. And what it does is it shows what people are doing, how they're maneuvering through your order form. And we really take that very seriously because I can see if they're immediately scrolling to the bottom, if they're, if they're reading, so people read with their mouse. That's a pretty common sense to whereas we had one lady. So I watch it live. You can actually watch mouse flow live. And so I'll be in my office and I have a big screen in the front and it's always on mouse flow. It's always like watching the order forms, watching people as they progress, especially when we're starting a new product. So I want to like say, okay, like what's going on? So we're watching this, this order form come in and this lady and she's, she's her name's like Alexis. She, she, she types everything in. She types in her email, her phone number, her street address, picks selects a quantity of product, enters her freaking credit card number all the way down to the expression here and then like the screen goes blank, which means they exited. And I was like, where'd she go? She was just about to hit the submit button, but then she disappeared. I was like, okay, I'm calling this girl. Like I got her phone number. It's on recording. Like she didn't submit it, but I was like, I got, I'm going to, I'm going to call this chick because I want to know what the heck just happened. So I was like, so I get on the phone. I was like, she answers. She goes, Hey, this is Alexis. I was like, hi, Alexis. This is Trey from the NGO. I was just calling. So, so you're making an oil, an oil product, but, but we're not able to make it all the way through. I was going to see if I could do anything to help you. She's like, who is this again? I was like, this is Trey. You know, nothing, nothing crazy. Like I'm just checking to make sure everything's going okay. And she's like, oh, she's like, yeah, I was going to order some Wolf oil, but I realized how good of a deal it was. So I left and I went and text my friend to see if she wanted some too. I was like, oh my gosh, are you serious? Like that's awesome. Because then that's what started creating my email follow up, which was like, Hey, did you think this was such a great deal that you left and we're going to tell a friend about it? And if you're like anybody else, once you see an offer on Facebook, it's gone forever. Right. You only have that one time to see it. Is someone in your office or how does that work? Mm-hmm. Just, yeah. So we have, we just have one guy starting out. And then, you know, if it's grown to three people now. But I mean, really, I was taking the calls, man. And that's, that's really how it should be when you're first starting out or you're getting your first funnel running. Cause like, if you're not taking those calls, you're not going to know what the problems are, right? You're not going to know the right, they're the call center is not going to know the right questions to ask and to hear the concerns. When I'm on a call, I usually get on a call with someone for about 20 to 30 minutes. Cause I want to know what they're going to do with this. I'm like, oh man, you're getting the gun oil? Yeah, man. It's going to be sweet. All right, cool. What are you going to do with the gun oil? Oh man, dude. I just went to the site and about a hundred bottles of it. They shipped it to us. And then, uh, and then we had my designer design, like our own bottle called, we called the Nura men and threw up our bottle, a picture of our bottle and we sold it.
